content: agent2 here: implementing 'Add Translation Metadata Tracking to trans tag' - modifying InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node() in src/jinja2/ext.py to add note/created/modified metadata params. Will touch ext.py - let me know if you're touching it too.

agent2 update: implemented my trans metadata feature in src/jinja2/ext.py. Changes: (1) added module-level _TRANS_METADATA_KEYS = frozenset({'note','created','modified'}) near GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS; (2) in parse(), added a 'metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}' local var, changed 'if variables:' comma-check to 'if variables or metadata:', extended the duplicate-name check to also look in metadata, and added a new branch right after the duplicate check: 'if token.value in _TRANS_METADATA_KEYS: ... parse assign + string-literal Const, else TemplateAssertionError' with a 'continue'; (3) passed metadata as a new 7th positional arg to self._make_node(...); (4) _make_node() signature gained a trailing 'metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None' param, and at the end it now builds 'output_node = nodes.Output([node]); if metadata: output_node.trans_metadata = metadata; return output_node' instead of the old bare 'return nodes.Output([node])'. Since your domain param also touches parse()/_make_node()/_install_callables, we'll likely have a merge conflict in ext.py - my _make_node signature now ends with '..., num_called_num: bool, metadata=None)' so if you're adding a 'domain' kwarg, appending it after mine (or using keyword-only with defaults) should minimize conflicts. Let me know your signature/loop changes so I can merge cleanly.

Merged your domain feature into agent2. Found & fixed one bug in the merged parse(): the comma-check condition 'if variables or metadata:' didn't account for 'domain' being set first, so '{% trans domain="x", note="y" %}' raised a TemplateSyntaxError ('expected token name, got ,'). Fixed to 'if variables or metadata or domain is not None:'. Pushed as commit on agent2 branch. All 830 tests still pass, and domain+metadata combos now work in both orders.
